sanskrit: बन्धुरात्माऽऽत्मनस्तस्य येनात्मैवात्मना जितः । अनात्मनस्तु शत्रुत्वे वर्तेतात्मैव शत्रुवत् ॥
hindiTranslation: जिसने अपनेआपसे अपनेआपको जीत लिया है, उसके लिये आप ही अपना बन्धु है और जिसने अपनेआपको नहीं जीता है, ऐसे अनात्माका आत्मा ही शत्रुतामें शत्रुकी तरह बर्ताव करता है ॥
englishTranslation: For one who has conquered the lower self by the true self, the true self is a friend. For one who has not conquered the lower self, the true self behaves like an enemy.
essence: The same inner nature that frees you can also fight you.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: When you master your restless impulses, your deepest nature supports you. If you do not, your own mind and habits work against you.
simpleInsight: Self-mastery feels like relief because the part of you that fought you starts helping.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is still frozen, and Krishna keeps turning the lesson inward. After saying the self can be friend or enemy, Krishna now sharpens it: victory over the lower self changes everything. The real battle is inside.
modernRelevance: You sit alone after sending the message you were afraid to send. If you acted from clarity, something in you steadies. If you acted from fear, the same mind turns harsh and restless.
translationLiteral: बन्धुः (friend) / आत्मा (the true self) / आत्मनः (of the self) / तस्य (of that one) / येन (by whom) / आत्मा (the true self) / एव (indeed) / आत्मना (by the true self) / जितः (conquered) / अनात्मनः (of one not mastered) / तु (but) / शत्रुत्वे (in hostility) / वर्तेत (acts) / आत्मा (the true self) / एव (indeed) / शत्रुवत् (like an enemy)
